# Code of Conduct

## Our Standards

This project is developed as part of a BITS Pilani Practice School (PS-1)
internship at Instruworks LLC FZ. Everyone contributing: students,
supervisors, and reviewers — is expected to help keep collaboration
constructive and professional.

Examples of expected behavior:
- Giving and accepting constructive feedback gracefully
- Verifying claims (about bugs, fixes, or data) before merging, rather than
  taking a description at face value
- Being respectful of differing skill levels and experience with git/tooling
- Focusing on what's best for the project and the team's shared deadline

Examples of unacceptable behavior:
- Personal attacks, insulting or derogatory comments
- Publishing others' private information without permission
- Submitting work that misrepresents what it actually does or what was
  actually tested

## Scope

This applies within all project spaces (this repository, related
communications about the project) and when someone is representing the
project in other settings.

## Enforcement

Concerns can be raised directly with the project's Architecture Lead or
with the supervising engineer at Instruworks LLC FZ.
